title: The Broken Field
content:
My soul is a dark ploughed field
In the cold rain;
My soul is a broken field
Ploughed by pain.

Where grass and bending flowers
Were growing,
The field lies broken now
For another sowing.

Great Sower when you tread
My field again,
Scatter the furrows there
With better grain.
meaning:
Sara Teasdale's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "The Broken Field"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
